# Pandas - sorting



```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd

# Sample data frame
data = pd.DataFrame({
    'Name': ['Alice', 'Tom', 'Charlie'],
    'Age': [25, 42, 35],
    'Salary': [50000, 60000, 70000]
})

# Sorting by the 'Age' column
s1 = data.sort_values(by='Age')
print(s1)
# Sorting in reverse order
s2 = data.sort_values(by='Salary', ascending=False)
print(s2)
# Sorting by 'Age' ascending, then 'Salary' descending
s3 = data.sort_values(by=['Age', 'Salary'], ascending=[True, False])
print(s3)

```

```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd

# Sample data frame
data = pd.DataFrame({
    'Name': ['Alice', 'Tom', 'Charlie'],
    'Age': [25, 41, 35],
    'Salary': [50000, 60000, 70000]
})

# Inplace sorting (replacing the existing variable) - currently not recommended
data.sort_values(by='Age', inplace=True)
print(data)
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd

df2 = pd.DataFrame({
    'Name': ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie', 'Dave'],
    'Age': [25, 30, None, 35],
    'Salary': [50000, None, 70000, 60000]
})

# Sorting with NaN at the end
s2 = df2.sort_values(by='Age', na_position='last')
print(s2)
# Sorting with NaN at the beginning
s3 = df2.sort_values(by='Age', na_position='first')
print(s3)
```

```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd

df3 = pd.DataFrame({
    'Name': ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie', 'Dave'],
    'Age': [25, 30, None, 35],
    'Salary': [50000, None, 70000, 60000]
})

s3 = df3.sort_values(by='Name', key=lambda x: x.str.len())
print(s3)
```
